---
title: "Extending cositos"
---

> **Who this section is for:** engineers **building a backend** — porting cositos to a new
> Jupyter kernel language, or contributing to the project internals. **We assume** you know
> a language ecosystem and are comfortable reading protocols and specifications. Unlike the
> [Using](../using/index.qmd) section, pages here *may* assume the widget vocabulary
> (comm, ESM, trait, reducer, buffer) — but each is defined once in the
> [Glossary](../using/glossary.qmd).

## The shape of a port

A cositos backend is three things:

1. A **Transport** adapter over your kernel's comm API.
2. The **pure protocol core** — message builders + buffer split/merge (~150 lines, fully
   specified by the golden fixtures in `fixtures/*.json`).
3. The **lifecycle shell** — a thin bridge that runs the pure lifecycle reducer's effects.

You write **no JavaScript**: you reuse anywidget's published `AnyModel`/`AnyView` frontend.

## Existing reference ports

Before you start, check whether a port already exists — you may only need to finish the
Transport adapter:

| Language | Status | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Python | complete (live) | `src/cositos/` |
| Julia | complete (live) | `julia/` |
| Clojure | complete (live, via Clay) | `clojure/` |
| C# | protocol core complete; live Transport pending | `csharp/Core.cs`, `csharp/README.md` |
| R | protocol core complete; live blocked upstream | `r/core.R`, `r/README.md` |

## How-to

- [**Porting guide**](porting.md) — implement + certify a backend in a new language.
- [**Local hooks & gates**](hooks.md) — set up the repo's pre-commit/pre-push checks.

## Reference & explanation

- [**Specifications**](specs.qmd) — the normative contracts (protocol, serialization,
  embed, lifecycle, lifecycle-shell) the test suite and every port certify against.
- [**Architecture**](architecture.qmd) — the lifecycle reducer, Transport seam, and
  Document model, and *why* they are shaped this way.
- [**Polyglot parity**](polyglot-parity.qmd) — how ports are pinned to identical output.
